data_in.description: Unaligned MS2 spectra files in matchms-compatible format (MGF, mzML, or mzXML)
Repository-provided example datasets from mandelbrot-project/memo_publication_examples or mandelbrot-project/memo

data_out.description: MemoMatrix object: a structured sample-by-feature matrix with MS2 fingerprint counts indexed by sample and peak/loss identifiers
Matrix metadata including sample identifiers, feature annotations, and alignment statistics

domain_knowledge: MS2 fingerprints are generated by counting occurrences of discrete MS2 peaks and neutral losses (mass differences to precursor ion) within each sample rather than aligning spectra by retention time.

MEMO's alignment operates at the sample level after fingerprint generation, enabling comparison of samples with strong RT shifts or acquired on different LC methods and mass spectrometer technologies.

The MemoMatrix output is a structured two-dimensional object with samples as rows and MS2 features (peaks/losses) as columns, containing count or intensity values suitable for downstream filtering and visualization.
matchms and spec2vec are core dependencies for spectrum parsing and document-style feature extraction; numpy and scikit-bio provide numerical and bioinformatic utilities.

direct_checks: verify file exists: locate unaligned MS2 spectra input file(s) in mandelbrot-project/memo or mandelbrot-project/memo_publication_examples repositories
script_runs: execute memo_from_unaligned() function from memo package on input spectra without errors
file_format_is: confirm output MemoMatrix object is serializable to standard Python pickle or HDF5 format
field_present: verify returned MemoMatrix contains 'memo_matrix' attribute as core data structure

row_count_equals or value_in_range: validate row count (samples) and column count (m/z features or neutral losses) are consistent with input file cardinality; no canonical answer without seeing test fixture
contains_substring: verify unit test references in changelog (test for memo_from_unaligned) exist in repository test suite and pass
output_matches_reference: compare generated MemoMatrix dimensions and sparsity pattern against expected outputs from repo test files, robust to minor floating-point precision differences
expert_review: examine whether MemoMatrix correctly represents MS2 peak and neutral loss counting as documented ('occurrence of MS2 peaks and neutral losses in each sample is counted')
validate that fingerprint alignment/comparison semantics are preserved in output matrix structure
confirm matrix values fall within expected range for occurrence counts (non-negative integers or normalized frequencies)

cmd: import matchms; from memo import memo_from_unaligned; import numpy as np; spectra = matchms.importing.load_from_mgf('{inputs.unaligned_spectra}'); matrix = memo_from_unaligned(spectra); print(f'MemoMatrix shape: {matrix.data.shape}'); print(f'Samples: {len(matrix.sample_ids)}'); print(f'Features: {len(matrix.feature_ids)}'); assert matrix.data.shape[0] > 0 and matrix.data.shape[1] > 0, 'Matrix is empty'; print('Validation passed: MemoMatrix successfully generated with non-empty feature counts and sample labels')

methodology_summary: Load and parse unaligned MS2 spectra files using matchms to extract fragmentation peaks and precursor masses.
Count occurrences of MS2 peaks and neutral losses (mass differences to precursor) within each sample to construct MS2 fingerprints.
Invoke memo_from_unaligned to align fingerprints across samples and generate the MemoMatrix object.
Validate matrix structure: verify sample-by-feature dimensionality, presence of metadata attributes, and consistency with unit test expectations.

title: Reproduce the MemoMatrix produced by memo_from_unaligned on the example dataset

Synthesis grounding: the following tools/outputs were NOT found in the source paper and are inferred — verify before use: Python 3.8, numpy, Matrix metadata including sample identifiers, feature annotations, and alignment statistics.
workflow_description: 1. Load unaligned MS2 spectra files in a format supported by matchms (e.g., .mgf, .mzML, .mzXML). 2. Parse spectra using matchms to extract MS2 fragmentation data and precursor information. 3. Count occurrences of MS2 peaks and neutral losses (mass differences to precursor) across all spectra in each sample to generate MS2 fingerprints. 4. Execute memo_from_unaligned function from memo-ms package to align fingerprints and construct the MemoMatrix object. 5. Inspect the resulting MemoMatrix for correct dimensionality, sample-by-feature structure, and presence of metadata attributes.
